body.title: [sprint] CodingDomain run-record hops carry model='' — the glance-view is blind exactly where the builder milestone needs it
body.description:
T-inference-run-record shipped with LoopResult.model/source_kind populated ONLY by GeneralDomain._run_attempt. CodingDomain runs through AgenticLoop (and the architect/editor split), which never sets them — so every coding hop records model='' and source_kind=''.

CONSEQUENCE: the run record cannot answer 'which model built this?' for the coding domain. That is the one domain the background-builder milestone actually depends on (see memory: project_background_builder_milestone — a builder you cannot read is a builder you cannot let run unattended). The glance-view is degraded precisely where it is load-bearing.

SHAPE: AgenticLoop already holds the last InferenceResponse per turn. Carry model/source_kind onto the LoopResult it returns. A multi-turn attempt may touch one model (the tier is resolved per attempt, not per turn) — assert that, do not assume it: if turns within one attempt can differ in model, the field must become a set, and THAT is a finding worth recording.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/agentic_loop.py, domains/architect_editor.py
**Design rules:** measure-dont-declare (verify whether a multi-turn attempt is single-model before collapsing it to one field)
**Scope boundary:** IN — populating model/source_kind for the coding path. OUT — the io join (T-run-record-io-join).
**Test plan:** hermetic, red->green. Drive CodingDomain (architect_editor disabled) with a stub device whose response reports model='stub-coder'; assert the run record's hop names it. RED: model is ''. THE DISCRIMINATOR: a multi-turn attempt (two tool-calling turns then a terminal) must still name the model — a fix that only reads the FIRST response would pass a single-turn test and fail this one.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
